report_name: TuxBot v3: New IoT Botnet Framework Developed with LLM Assistance

threat_actors.name: TuxBot v3 Developer
threat_actors.description: An unnamed developer, assessed to be operating from Iran, who created the TuxBot v3 Evolution botnet framework, notably using a large language model (LLM) for code generation.

threat_actors.name: Keksec
threat_actors.description: A malware ecosystem whose infrastructure is shared with the TuxBot v3 botnet, as indicated by a hard-coded IP address.

attack_patterns.name: Generative AI
attack_patterns.description: The developer of TuxBot v3 used a large language model (LLM) to generate code for the botnet framework. This automated approach introduced several bugs and non-functional components into the malware.

attack_patterns.name: Password Guessing
attack_patterns.description: TuxBot v3's primary infection vector is brute-forcing Telnet credentials on exposed IoT devices using a hard-coded list of 1,496 common and default credential pairs.

attack_patterns.name: Network Service Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The TuxBot v3 bot scans for open Telnet, SSH, HTTP, and ADB ports on IoT devices to identify potential targets for infection.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The TuxBot v3 framework includes non-functional exploit modules for over 30 IoT device families, indicating an intended but currently broken capability to exploit public-facing applications.

attack_patterns.name: Unix Shell
attack_patterns.description: The botnet likely uses shell commands to execute payloads and achieve persistence on the compromised Linux-based IoT devices.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: TuxBot v3 uses web protocols for command and control communication, as well as for retrieving updates or payloads.

attack_patterns.name: Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The botnet uses legitimate public web services, specifically Pastebin and Telegram, as a fallback mechanism to retrieve C2 server information if the primary C2 is unavailable.

attack_patterns.name: Domain Generation Algorithms
attack_patterns.description: TuxBot v3 employs a Domain Generation Algorithm (DGA) as a fallback mechanism to dynamically find new C2 servers for resilient communication.

attack_patterns.name: Network Denial of Service
attack_patterns.description: The primary function and ultimate goal of the TuxBot v3 botnet is to launch Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks against specified targets.

malware.name: TuxBot v3 Evolution
malware.description: A modular IoT botnet framework designed for DDoS attacks, partially developed using an LLM. It infects devices by brute-forcing Telnet credentials and is derived from the AISURU and Wuhan botnet families.

malware.name: AISURU
malware.description: A known botnet family from which TuxBot v3 Evolution is derived.

malware.name: Wuhan botnet
malware.description: A previously unknown botnet family from which TuxBot v3 Evolution is derived.

malware.name: Kaitori
malware.description: A malware family associated with the Keksec ecosystem, which shares infrastructure with TuxBot v3.

tools.name: Large Language Model
tools.description: A large language model was used by the developer to generate code for the TuxBot v3 framework, resulting in a partially functional but viable botnet.

tools.name: MHDDoS
tools.description: An open-source Python DDoS toolkit from which the TuxBot v3 framework's DDoS attack methods were partially ported.

tools.name: Pastebin
tools.description: Used as a C2 fallback mechanism for the TuxBot v3 botnet to retrieve C2 server information.

tools.name: Telegram
tools.description: Used as a C2 fallback mechanism for the TuxBot v3 botnet to retrieve C2 server information.

tools.name: Go
tools.description: The Go programming language was used to write the C2 server component of the TuxBot v3 framework.

tools.name: Docker
tools.description: The TuxBot v3 source code included a Docker configuration for automated deployment, indicating a structured development process.

tools.name: QEMU
tools.description: The TuxBot v3 source code included a QEMU test directory, indicating a structured development and testing process.

identities.name: Arvan Cloud
identities.description: An Iranian cloud and CDN provider that hosts the parent domain for the TuxBot v3 developer's infrastructure.

identities.name: Unit 42
identities.description: The security research organization that analyzed and reported on the TuxBot v3 Evolution botnet.

infrastructure.name: TuxBot v3 C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The command and control server infrastructure for the TuxBot v3 botnet, using TCP ports 1999 and 31337 for bot communication.

infrastructure.name: TuxBot v3 Developer Domain
infrastructure.description: A domain (newtuxdev.sevielw.digikalas.online) used by the developer, hosted on Iran's Arvan Cloud CDN, and linked to the botnet's development.

infrastructure.name: Keksec/Kaitori Shared Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure, identified by the IP address 185.10.68.127, that is shared between the TuxBot v3 botnet and the Keksec/Kaitori malware ecosystem.

indicators.name: TuxBot v3 C2 Infrastructure IOCs
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: TuxBot v3 C2 Communication
id: 7c8d1e2f-3a4b-4c5d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to IP addresses and DNS queries for domains associated with the TuxBot v3 IoT botnet C2 infrastructure.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/tuxbot-v3-evolution-iot-botnet/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-15
tags:
  - attack.t1071
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_ip:
    DestinationIp:
      - '185.10.68.127'
  condition: selection_ip
--- 
title: TuxBot v3 C2 DNS Query
id: 8f9a0b1c-2d3e-4f5a-b6c7-d8e9f0a1b2c3
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries for domains associated with the TuxBot v3 IoT botnet C2 infrastructure.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/tuxbot-v3-evolution-iot-botnet/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-15
tags:
  - attack.t1071
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_domain:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'newtuxdev.sevielw.digikalas.online'
      - 'digikalas.online'
  condition: selection_domain
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to known TuxBot v3 C2 infrastructure, including a shared IP and developer domains.

indicators.name: TuxBot Telnet Brute-Force Scanning
indicators.pattern:
title: TuxBot Telnet Brute-Force Scanning
id: 4a5b6c7d-8e9f-4a1b-8c3d-4e5f6a7b8c9d
status: experimental
description: Detects a high volume of outbound connection attempts on TCP port 23 (Telnet), which is indicative of TuxBot's primary infection vector.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/tuxbot-v3-evolution-iot-botnet/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-15
tags:
  - attack.t1110.001
  - attack.credential_access
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationPort: 23
    Protocol: 'tcp'
  condition: selection | count() > 50 by SourceIp within 1 minute
indicators.description: Detects high-volume outbound connection attempts on TCP port 23 (Telnet), characteristic of TuxBot's brute-force scanning behavior.

indicators.name: TuxBot C2 Communication Ports
indicators.pattern:
title: TuxBot C2 Communication Ports
id: 9f0a1b2c-3d4e-4f5a-b6c7-d8e9f0a1b2c3
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound TCP connections on ports 1999 or 31337 from IoT devices, which are used for TuxBot C2 communication.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/tuxbot-v3-evolution-iot-botnet/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-15
tags:
  - attack.t1071
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_connection
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationPort:
      - 1999
      - 31337
    Protocol: 'tcp'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects outbound TCP traffic on ports 1999 or 31337, which are the default and development C2 ports for TuxBot v3.

indicators.name: TuxBot C2 Fallback via Web Services
indicators.pattern:
title: TuxBot C2 Fallback via Web Services
id: 5b6c7d8e-9f0a-4b2c-8d4e-5f6a7b8c9d0e
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries from IoT devices to Telegram or Pastebin, which are used by TuxBot as C2 fallback mechanisms.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/tuxbot-v3-evolution-iot-botnet/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-15
tags:
  - attack.t1102
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith:
      - '.api.telegram.org'
      - '.pastebin.com'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - 'telegram.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects connections from IoT devices to Pastebin or Telegram APIs, which TuxBot uses as a C2 fallback channel.

relationships.source_name: TuxBot C2 Fallback via Web Services
